title: Assessment Response
description: The structured payload returned by the shard per command invocation. This is the OAP ESCALATE evidence record for shell commands.

command.description: The parsed command that was assessed.

raw.description: The original unmodified command string as submitted.

binary.description: The resolved binary name after multiplexer unwrapping if applicable.

binary_raw.description: The binary name as written, before multiplexer unwrapping. Omitted if no unwrapping occurred.

flags.description: Parsed flags, each expanded to canonical form if abbreviated.

raw.description: Flag as written in the command.

canonical.description: Canonical form after abbreviation expansion. Equal to raw if no expansion occurred.

abbreviated.description: True if this flag was expanded from an abbreviation.

decision.description: The assessment decision. allow: safe under configured policy, no confirmation needed. escalate: present to user for confirmation (OAP ESCALATE). deny: hard block — sandbox escape or fail-closed condition.

risk_tags.description: Union of all risk tags matched across all fired rules.

likely_consequences.description: Union of likely_consequences from all fired rules. User-facing outcome categories for non-technical confirmation UX.

severity.description: Highest severity across all matched rules.

overall_risk.description: Derived overall risk level for host UX. critical: DENY decision or non-bypassable tag present. high: ERROR severity or irreversible+recursive. medium: WARNING severity. low: INFO only.

reversible.description: Most conservative reversibility across all matched rules. 'no' takes precedence over 'depends' over 'yes'.

constraint_violations.description: Constraints evaluated at runtime that were violated. Empty if none.

constraint.description: The constraint that was violated. 'escapes-sandbox': path resolves outside sandbox root — always DENY. 'escapes-allowed-tools': binary not in allowed list — ESCALATE. 'line-continuation': bare newline or backslash-newline outside quoted strings — ESCALATE. 'requires-confirmation': policy requires human approval regardless of risk tags.

detail.description: Human-readable explanation. E.g. 'Path /etc resolves outside sandbox boundary /home/user/project'.

non_bypassable_tags.description: Subset of risk_tags that are non-bypassable — always force ESCALATE or DENY regardless of policy. Populated when any of: executes-code, irreversible, escapes-sandbox are present.

matched_rules.description: The rules from the committed ruleset that fired. Provides traceability from decision to rule.

rule_id.description: The id field from the matched rule.

matched_pattern.description: The specific pattern within the rule that matched.

ruleset_tool.description: The tool name of the ruleset this rule came from.

inspection_findings.description: Findings from deep argument inspection (R-INS-01 through R-INS-07) that are not covered by ruleset matches. Each is a named signal with detail.

elevated_to.description: Risk tag this finding elevated the assessment to, if any.

persistence_signal.description: Present when the same risk category has been attempted multiple times in this session after a prior block or denial. Lightweight observable for capability tunneling patterns.

risk_tag.description: The risk category being repeatedly attempted.

attempt_count.description: Number of times this risk category has been attempted in the current session window after a block.

session_window_seconds.description: The session window over which attempts are counted.

tool_known.description: True if a committed ruleset exists for this binary. False triggers ESCALATE regardless of other findings.

ruleset_version.description: Version identifier of the committed ruleset used. Included in audit records.

assessment_latency_ms.description: Wall-clock milliseconds from command receipt to response. For performance monitoring against R-NFN-01.

oap_evidence.description: Optional. OAP-compatible evidence record for deployments using Open Agent Passport. Wraps this response for attachment to an OAP ESCALATE decision.
